items.label: Development Complete — Schemas
messages: Intent: Now I understand the codebase structure. The feature is `health-check-ci` — I need to create health check schemas for a CI-compatible health endpoint. Let me check for any existing references and the backend structure.
Good — no existing health check code. This is a greenfield schema. Let me now create the health check schema and tests.
Now I have full context. Let me create the health check schema file, update the barrel export, and add tests.
Now add the test blocks at the end of the test file:
All 55 tests pass (30 existing + 25 new). Build succeeds. Now let me run the post-change review and commit:
Review confirms: **no breaking changes**, minimal structural impact (1.1% footprint), purely additive. Now commit and complete the pipeline:

**Schema development complete.** Here's a summary of what was implemented:  ### New Schemas Created  **`packages/schemas/src/health.ts`** — Health check endpoint schemas for `GET /health`:  | Schema | Type | Purpose | |--------|------|---------| | `HealthStatusSchema` | `z.enum(["healthy", "degraded", "unhealthy"])` | Shared status enum for overall and per-check status | | `HealthCheckEntrySchema` | `z.object(...)` | Individual dependency check result (`name`, `status`, optional `message`, optional `durationMs`) | | `HealthCheckResponseSchema` | `z.object(...)` | Top-level response (`status`, `timestamp`, optional `version`, optional `checks[]`) |  ### Design Decisions - **Additive only** — no existing schemas were modified; zero breaking changes confirmed by Roam review - **Three-tier status** — `healthy`/`degraded`/`unhealthy` supports CI (binary pass/fail on `unhealthy`) and monitoring (gradual degradation visibility) - **Optional `checks` array** — allows the backend to include per-dependency breakdowns (database, cache, etc.) without requiring them for a simple liveness probe - **`durationMs` with nonnegative constraint** — enables latency monitoring per dependency - **Follows existing patterns** — ISO-8601 datetime validation, `.min(1)` for required strings, consistent JSDoc examples  ### Test Coverage - **25 new tests** added covering all 3 schemas with valid inputs, edge cases (zero duration, empty arrays), and invalid input rejection - **All 55 tests pass** (30 existing + 25 new), build succeeds cleanly

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh backend "feat(schemas): add health check endpoint schemas (HealthStatus, HealthCheckEntry, HealthCheckResponse)" 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:complete health-check-ci schema-dev 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark Development Complete — Schemas" 2>&1

items.label: Infrastructure Written — Terraform
messages: Now let me check the existing API specs and remaining infra files to fully understand the pattern:

Now I have a complete understanding. Let me review the spec's infrastructure section carefully:  **Key finding from the spec:** > **Infra:** No Terraform changes — the environment variable is injected by CI/CD, not IaC.  The health check endpoint (`GET /api/health`) is: 1. `authLevel: "anonymous"` — accessible without function keys or APIM auth 2. Uses `STRICT_HEALTH_MODE` env var injected by CI/CD (`az functionapp config appsettings set`), not Terraform 3. Does not require APIM routing — it's designed for direct infrastructure probe access  Let me verify the existing infrastructure is still valid by running the Terraform validation gate:
Terraform plan succeeded with no new resources — only minor drift changes on existing resources. The plan confirms no new infrastructure is needed for this feature.  Now let me complete the pipeline:

Infrastructure phase complete. Here's a summary:  ## ✅ Infrastructure Written — Terraform (No Changes Required)  **Decision:** No new Terraform resources needed for the `health-check-ci` feature.  **Rationale (per SPEC):** - The `GET /api/health` endpoint uses `authLevel: "anonymous"` on the Azure Function App — it's designed for direct infrastructure probe access, bypassing APIM authentication - The `STRICT_HEALTH_MODE` environment variable is injected by CI/CD (`az functionapp config appsettings set` in `deploy-backend.yml`), not by Terraform — this is intentional to prove the self-healing loop from integration test failure through CI/CD reset - No new data-plane resources (databases, caches, queues) are provisioned  **Validation:** - `terraform init` ✅ - `terraform validate` ✅   - `terraform plan -var-file=dev.tfvars` ✅ — Plan: 0 to add, 0 to destroy (only minor drift on existing resources)  **Existing infrastructure unchanged:** | Resource | Name | Status | |----------|------|--------| | Function App | `func-sample-app-001` | Unchanged | | APIM Gateway | `apim-sample-app-001` | Unchanged | | Key Vault | `kv-sampleapp-001` | Unchanged | | Static Web App | `swa-sample-app-001` | Unchanged |

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/infra && az account show > /dev/null 2>&1; echo "Azure auth: $?"

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:doc-note health-check-ci infra-architect "No new Terraform resources required for health-check-ci feature. Per SPEC: 'Infra: No Terraform ch
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:complete health-check-ci infra-architect 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark Infrastructure Written — Terraform" 2>&1

items.label: Draft PR Created
messages: No existing PR found, and the spec file is named `system-health_SPEC.md`. Let me read the available feature context to generate a proper PR title.
Good. I have all the context I need. The feature is about a system health check endpoint with CI integration. No existing PR exists. Let me create the draft PR.
Draft PR #33 created successfully. Now let me update the pipeline state.
All done. Let me clean up the temp file.

## ✅ Draft PR Created Successfully  | Item | Value | |------|-------| | **PR** | [#33](https://github.com/RomanKaliupinMelonusa/DAGent-t/pull/33) | | **Title** | `feat(health-check-ci): add system health endpoint with CI-injected status` | | **Status** | Draft | | **Base** | `main` ← `feature/health-check-ci` | | **Pipeline step** | `create-draft-pr` marked **done** |  **What happened:** 1. Checked for existing PRs on `feature/health-check-ci` — none found 2. Generated a PR body describing the feature and next steps (Terraform plan review → `/dagent approve-infra`) 3. Created draft PR #33 via `gh pr create --draft` 4. Updated pipeline state: marked step complete, set PR URL, added note 5. Committed state via `agent-commit.sh pipeline`

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && gh pr list --head "feature/health-check-ci" --json number -q '.[0].number' 2>/dev/null || echo ""
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | sed 's|.*github.com[:/]||;s|\.git$||'
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && gh pr list --head "feature/health-check-ci" --json number -q '.[0].number' 2>/dev/null | cat; echo "EXIT:$?"
find /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app -name "*SPEC*" -o -name "*spec*" 2>/dev/null | head -10; echo "---"; ls /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/in-progress/ 2>/dev/null || echo "no in-progress
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:complete health-check-ci create-draft-pr 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:set-note health-check-ci "Draft PR #33 created — awaiting Terraform plan" 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark Draft PR Created" 2>&1
